title: Hybrid scorer per-task Optuna retune

queries: hyperparameter retune after architectural change test subset model selection source
task-specific hyperparameter retuning after replay-confirmed model change

papers.title: N/A
papers.claim: This is a post-screening retune stage rather than a new modeling family.
papers.relevance: Used after hybrid scorer replay confirmation to search task-specific best settings under the fixed Stage 3 subset protocol.

evidence.why_now: EXP099 made the hybrid scorer promotable on subset replay, while EXP102 showed that fixed baseline hyperparameters are not sufficient for a fair full-test verdict. This retune was launched as a transitional check before the later complexity review retired the exhaustive hybrid head from the final scalable path.

hypothesis: The hybrid scorer family might need task-specific hyperparameter retune after the structural change. Per-task Optuna search under the same 512-step test-subset protocol was used as a transitional validation step before the exhaustive hybrid head was retired on scalability grounds.
notes: Per-task retune is allowed for this stage; final keep/drop still remains a representative-task bundle judgment.
This bundle was stopped after the complexity review concluded that the current exhaustive autocomplete hybrid head scales as O(L*C) and should not remain on the final scalable mainline.
Treat the hybrid scorer as a valuable attempt and evidence source, but do not continue this specific retune path as the final implementation route.
